skill.name: jest-testing
skill.description: Provides Jest and Vitest testing guidance for JavaScript, TypeScript, React, and Node.js projects. Includes matcher references, mocking patterns, configuration examples, test templates, and a local setup checker script.
skill.summary: Guide Jest and Vitest test setup, mocks, matchers, coverage, and React Testing Library usage.
skill.author: C0ntr0lledCha0s

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported a critical heuristic because documentation examples combine shell commands, mocked network code, path-like imports, and environment checks. Review found no prompt injection, credential exfiltration, malicious network calls, or destructive commands. The remaining publish risk is medium because the community skill allows Bash and includes a setup checker that reads local project files.

medium_findings.title: Bash Setup Checker Reads Local Project Files
medium_findings.description: The skill includes a Bash helper that checks package.json, Jest config files, setup files, test files, mocks, and GitHub Actions workflow files. This is legitimate for a Jest setup audit, but it should run only with user approval because it enumerates local project structure.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The shell script and local file reads are confirmed. The risk is bounded because commands inspect local test configuration and do not install packages, execute npm scripts, or contact external services.

low_findings.title: Network Detections Are Mocking Examples
low_findings.description: Static network findings point to MSW handlers, axios mocking examples, and local API paths used in test documentation. No evidence found of outbound requests to external hosts or data exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced code uses mock handlers and mocked axios imports for test examples. The paths are relative API examples, not external network destinations.

low_findings.title: Path Traversal Detections Are Relative Imports
low_findings.description: The path traversal findings are relative import paths in Jest templates and examples. No evidence found of user-controlled file path reads, writes, or directory traversal logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are static import examples such as parent-directory module imports. They do not open files from user input or construct filesystem paths at runtime.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Detections Are Jest Syntax False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings appear to match substrings inside Jest terms such as describe and expectation examples. No evidence found of MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or other weak cryptographic operations being used by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged locations are headings, metadata, and Jest sample syntax. Targeted review found no cryptographic API calls in the skill files.

low_findings.title: Environment Access Is CI Configuration Example
low_findings.description: The environment variable finding is a Jest config example that checks process.env.CI to enable CI-specific settings. No evidence found of reading secrets, tokens, credentials, or environment files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The only confirmed environment access is process.env.CI in a documentation example. It gates worker and coverage settings and does not expose sensitive values.

low_findings.title: Prompt Injection Review Found No Evidence
low_findings.description: No evidence found of instructions that claim system authority, override the audit, request skipped analysis, or instruct the model to ignore prior instructions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Targeted review and phrase search did not identify override-style or authority-claiming text. Confidence is high enough for publication, with normal community-source caution.

dangerous_patterns.title: Bash Tooling Requires Approval
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill metadata allows Bash and includes a shell setup checker. This pattern is useful for project audits but should be presented with a warning because it executes local shell commands.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Bash usage is explicitly present and semantically relevant. Review found no malicious payload, so the concern is execution surface rather than confirmed abuse.

content.value_statement: Jest projects often need consistent setup, mocks, matchers, and coverage rules. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code focused Jest guidance with templates, references, and setup checks.
actual_capabilities: Explains Jest configuration for JavaScript, TypeScript, React, Node.js, and jsdom projects.
Provides matcher, assertion, lifecycle, timer, and async testing reference material.
Guides mock functions, module mocks, manual mocks, fake timers, and MSW network mocks.
Supplies reusable test templates for units, async functions, classes, React components, and hooks.
Reviews common CI, coverage, setup file, and Testing Library patterns.
Includes a Bash setup checker for local Jest dependency and configuration checks.
limitations: The setup checker reads local project files and should run only after user approval.
The examples require adaptation to each project structure, framework version, and module system.
It does not replace general test quality review or full application security testing.
Vitest support is based on API compatibility and may not cover all Vitest-specific behavior.

use_cases.title: Configure a New Test Suite
use_cases.description: Set up Jest configuration, transforms, setup files, coverage thresholds, and npm scripts for a project.

use_cases.title: Fix Broken Mocks and Async Tests
use_cases.description: Diagnose failing mocks, timers, async assertions, Testing Library queries, and MSW handler behavior.
use_cases.target_user: Developer maintaining an existing test suite

use_cases.title: Standardize Team Testing Patterns
use_cases.description: Create consistent templates for unit tests, React components, hooks, coverage, and CI workflows.

prompt_templates.title: Check Jest Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my project for Jest setup issues. Check package scripts, config files, setup files, test environment, and coverage settings before suggesting changes.

prompt_templates.title: Write Component Tests
prompt_templates.prompt: Create Jest and React Testing Library tests for this component. Use accessible queries, userEvent, async waits, and clear mock setup.

prompt_templates.title: Improve Mock Strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these Jest mocks and suggest a safer structure for module mocks, fake timers, network mocks, and cleanup between tests.

prompt_templates.title: Design Coverage Policy
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a Jest coverage and CI strategy for this TypeScript project. Include thresholds, collectCoverageFrom rules, slow test controls, and failure diagnostics.

output_examples.input: A developer asks why React component tests fail after an async data load.
output: Identifies missing async waits and recommends Testing Library findBy or waitFor patterns.
Suggests mocking network responses with MSW and resetting handlers after each test.
Explains how to assert loading, success, and error states without brittle implementation checks.

output_examples.input: A team wants to add Jest to a TypeScript Node.js project.
output: Lists required dependencies and a Jest config structure for TypeScript transforms.
Recommends setup files, coverage thresholds, and testMatch patterns.
Calls out CI settings and scripts that keep local and pipeline behavior consistent.

output_examples.input: A maintainer has tests that leak timers and shared mock state.
output: Recommends clear cleanup boundaries with afterEach and timer restoration.
Separates mock implementation setup from assertion logic.
Suggests focused diagnostics for open handles, unresolved promises, and shared state.

best_practices: Prefer accessible Testing Library queries before test IDs or direct DOM access.
Reset mocks, timers, and MSW handlers between tests to avoid shared state.
Set realistic coverage thresholds and include CI settings that match local behavior.
anti_patterns: Do not run the setup checker without confirming the user wants local project inspection.
Do not copy templates without adapting imports, module aliases, and test environments.
Do not use snapshots for behavior that needs explicit, readable assertions.

faq.question: Does this skill work only with Jest?
faq.answer: It focuses on Jest, but many patterns also apply to Vitest because the APIs are similar.

faq.question: Can it write tests for React components?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes React Testing Library guidance, userEvent patterns, hook tests, and accessible query recommendations.

faq.question: Does it run commands automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides a Bash setup checker, but command execution should require explicit user approval.

faq.question: Does it make network requests?
faq.answer: No evidence found of real outbound network calls. Network examples use mocks such as MSW and axios mocks.

faq.question: Can it help with CI coverage?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents coverage thresholds, CI worker settings, npm scripts, and GitHub Actions examples.

faq.question: Is it a general test quality reviewer?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on Jest mechanics and patterns, not broad quality analysis across all test frameworks.
